tiktok.caption: Refusing a raise because of 'tax brackets'? You're leaving $8K/year on the table. Here's the math that proves the myth completely wrong.

youtube_short.title: The Tax Bracket Myth Costs You $8K/Year
title_alternatives: Why Turning Down a Raise Is an $8,000 Mistake
Tax Brackets Don't Work the Way You Think
youtube_short.description: Millions of workers turn down raises because they're afraid of moving into a higher tax bracket — but that fear is based on a completely false understanding of how taxes work. Tax brackets are marginal: only the dollars above each threshold get taxed at the higher rate. Even if your entire $5,000 raise fell in the 22% bracket, you'd still take home $3,900 more. You literally cannot earn more money and end up with less. Follow for more money psychology breakdowns that actually matter. #taxes #personalfinance #finance #salary #moneymindset

hook_line: This tax fear is making you refuse free money
